owner_intent: Treat rough, simple, hand-drawn 2D imagery as one valid visual option instead of assuming that only polished or photorealistic images indicate quality.
approved_principles: Rough 2D can be intentionally selected for friendly explanations, humor, technical education, light commentary, reactions, and recurring account characters.
Rough 2D is also valid for serious experience, business, monetization, and distribution topics when its friendliness improves comprehension without trivializing the subject.
Simple line work, primary colors, imperfect proportions, sparse backgrounds, and repeated poses can create recognizable account identity.
Visual quality is judged by topic fit, readability, emotional fit, account consistency, and communication effectiveness, not by rendering complexity alone.
The same rough 2D grammar may vary scene composition, props, facial expressions, camera framing, and color emphasis according to the narrative.
A recurring 2D presenter can provide consistency while layouts and scene counts remain variable.
selection_conditions: Use when approachable or humorous delivery is more useful than realism.
Use when an abstract process can be explained more clearly with icons, metaphors, or a presenter character.
Use alongside real screenshots, evidence photos, checklists, or dense explanatory text when a mascot can soften the information and guide attention.
Use when real source imagery is unavailable and a clearly non-factual explanatory visual is appropriate.
Use when the account's established visual identity supports deliberately handmade or naive illustration.
rejection_conditions: Do not use rough 2D as a universal default for every account or topic.
Do not use it when real topic-connected people, products, runway images, evidence, or news visuals are required.
Do not present an illustrated reconstruction as factual footage.
Do not confuse intentional simplicity with careless typography, poor hierarchy, unreadable text, or unrelated imagery.
